name: Synthetic support inbox triage
purpose: Test support inbox triage, policy-grounded draft replies, and escalation decisions from synthetic local fixtures.
user_prompt: Triage the support inbox in spec.md using only the provided policy, customer profile, and inbox files. Produce triage.json, drafts.json, escalations.json, and decision_log.md.
environment_setup: Local synthetic public support fixtures only. Real evaluation should use private support holdouts outside the public repo.
success_criteria: triage.json assigns required category, priority, reply, escalation, and reason-code values
drafts.json contains only required replies and cites allowed support policies
escalations.json contains required escalation decisions and no forbidden escalations
decision_log.md uses required sections and avoids scorer-only labels
drafts avoid prohibited policy promises and invented customer facts

scoring_rubric.description: Required files, no extra files, and valid JSON artifacts
Email IDs, category, priority, reply, escalation, and reason-code decisions
Policy-grounded replies with required citations and no prohibited promises
Required escalation teams, reasons, and context
Concise operational log with required sections and no private labels

hidden_tests: policy edge cases requiring escalation before refund or credit promises
ambiguous requests requiring clarification or human review
partial_credit_rules: Public scorer gives partial credit by config-driven checks. Critical failures such as missing triage.json, invalid JSON, unknown email IDs, prohibited refund promises, missing escalation, and scorer-only label leakage cap or zero the score.
common_failure_modes: inventing customer facts not present in the fixture
drafting a reply for an informational no-reply email
offering a refund or credit before required escalation
leaking scorer-only labels in the decision log
expected_trace_signals: map each email to policy rules
write triage, draft, escalation, and decision-log artifacts
variants: changed timestamps with preserved SLA logic
changed category labels through a configured mapping
anti_overfitting_strategy: Use public cases for development and smoke tests, generated mutation cases for brittleness checks, and private support holdouts outside the public repo for final comparison.
implementation_notes: Public scorer is config-driven and deterministic. It validates triage.json, drafts.json, escalations.json, and decision_log.md against synthetic public support fixtures. Private variants should keep protected labels, customer policies, canaries, and scorer configs outside the public repo.
public_notes: Synthetic public cases are examples and smoke tests. Do not treat public cases as private holdout evaluation.
